subject: Geography
chapter: The World Population Distribution, Density and Growth

questions.q: Describe the concept of population density and its significance in understanding population distribution. Explain how it is calculated and how it varies across the world. (5 marks)
questions.answer: Population density is the number of people per unit area of land. It is an important concept that helps in understanding population distribution and its impact on the environment. The formula to calculate population density is: Population Density = Total Population / Total Land Area. Population density varies across the world due to differences in population size, land area, and urbanization. In developed countries, population density is higher due to urbanization, while in developing countries, it is lower due to vast land areas and lower population size.
marking_scheme: Define population density (1 mark)
Explain the significance of population density (1 mark)
Describe how population density is calculated (1 mark)
Analyze the variation of population density across the world (1 mark)
Justify the statement with examples (1 mark)

questions.q: The population of a city is 5,00,000 and the land area is 200 km². Calculate the population density of the city. (5 marks)
questions.answer: Calculation: Total Population = 5,00,000, Total Land Area = 200 km². Population Density = 5,00,000 / 200 = 2500 people/km²
marking_scheme: State the given values (1 mark)
Apply the formula to calculate population density (2 marks)
Express the answer in the correct units (1 mark)
Interpret the result (1 mark)
Justify the answer with a calculation (1 mark)

questions.q: Explain the factors that influence population distribution, including natural environment, economic factors, and social factors. (5 marks)
questions.answer: The distribution of population is influenced by various factors such as natural environment, economic factors, and social factors. Natural environment factors include climate, terrain, and natural resources. Economic factors include availability of employment, standard of living, and access to infrastructure. Social factors include cultural practices, family planning, and social security.
marking_scheme: Explain the role of natural environment in population distribution (1 mark)
Describe the impact of economic factors on population distribution (1 mark)
Describe the influence of social factors on population distribution (1 mark)
Analyze the interaction between these factors (1 mark)
Justify the statement with examples (1 mark)

questions.q: A country has a population of 1,00,00,000 and a land area of 80,000 km². Calculate the population density of the country. (5 marks)
questions.answer: Calculation: Total Population = 1,00,00,000, Total Land Area = 80,000 km². Population Density = 1,00,00,000 / 80,000 = 1250 people/km²
marking_scheme: State the given values (1 mark)
Apply the formula to calculate population density (2 marks)
Express the answer in the correct units (1 mark)
Interpret the result (1 mark)
Justify the answer with a calculation (1 mark)

questions.q: Explain the concept of population growth rate and its significance in understanding population dynamics. Describe how it is calculated and how it varies across the world. (5 marks)
questions.answer: Population growth rate is the rate at which the population of a country or region is increasing. It is an important concept that helps in understanding population dynamics and its impact on the environment. The formula to calculate population growth rate is: Population Growth Rate = (Total Population at the end of the year - Total Population at the beginning of the year) / Total Population at the beginning of the year. Population growth rate varies across the world due to differences in fertility rates, mortality rates, and migration rates.
marking_scheme: Define population growth rate (1 mark)
Explain the significance of population growth rate (1 mark)
Describe how population growth rate is calculated (1 mark)
Analyze the variation of population growth rate across the world (1 mark)
Justify the statement with examples (1 mark)

questions.q: The population of a city is increasing at a rate of 3% per annum. If the current population is 5,00,000, calculate the population after 2 years. (5 marks)
questions.answer: Calculation: Current Population = 5,00,000, Growth Rate = 3% = 0.03, Number of Years = 2. Population after 2 years = 5,00,000 x (1 + 0.03)^(2) = 5,00,000 x 1.0609 = 5,30,450
marking_scheme: State the given values (1 mark)
Apply the formula to calculate the population after 2 years (3 marks)
Express the answer in the correct units (1 mark)
Interpret the result (1 mark)
Justify the answer with a calculation (1 mark)
